title: DARPA Records Related to Social Media Influence and Memetics Research

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: DARPA Records Related to Social Media Influence and Memetics Research
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to DARPA programs, research, and communications concerning social media analysis, memetics, and strategic communication.

Specifically, I request all records, including but not limited to reports, contracts, training materials, presentations, emails, briefing materials, and internal memoranda, from January 1, 2010 to the present that reference:

- The Social Media in Strategic Communication (SMISC) program
- “Memetic” or “memetics” research in the context of strategic communication or information operations
- Social media influence, narrative analysis, or information diffusion
- The detection, modeling, or countering of misinformation, disinformation, or online propaganda
- Social network analysis, sentiment analysis, or computational models of online discourse related to influence or persuasion

To the extent possible, I request that records be provided in electronic format.

If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please provide all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and indicate the specific FOIA exemption(s) applied.

I am a journalist requesting these records for public interest reporting. I request a waiver of all fees, as disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to public understanding of government activities.

Please contact me if you have any questions or need clarification that would help expedite processing.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lily Dokhanchi
